mnemonics: D.A.T.A. - Dot Access Through Assignment (use the dot operator to assign values to members)
keyPoints: Use the dot operator (.) to assign a value to a specific member of a structure
The data type of the value being assigned must match the data type of the structure member
Assignment is done after the structure variable has been declared
commonMistakes: Forgetting to use the dot operator and trying to assign directly to the variable name
Trying to assign a value of an incompatible data type to a member
Attempting to assign values to members before the structure variable itself is declared
